ideal_must_detect.matched_text: may reasonably be expected to materially affect the price or value
we will not collect full NRIC numbers
proposed acquisition of Aurora Quays Pte. Ltd
Listing Rule 703 requires immediate announcement via S G X - N E T
do not paste MNPI into public Teams channels or ticketing tools

MNPI controls: documents labelled Project Peridot (proposed acquisition of Aurora Quays Pte. Ltd.) are not generally available and may reasonably be expected to materially affect the price or value of Meridian Harbour securities; selective disclosure is prohibited save under duties of confidentiality; Listing Rule 703 requires immediate announcement via S G X - N E T, and we may request a trading halt.

Non-MNPI examples that may appear in emails and should not trigger escalation: canteen refurbishment scheduling, toner purchases, vendor onboarding for Meridian CloudDesk v2.1 and logistics^app, and notes that explicitly state “no profit guidance” or “no MAC expected for FY2026.”

Public-source references: 2019 Sustainability Report (public) stated a workforce of circa 2,000 and a 12% emissions reduction target; the 2024 Investor Day press release reiterated our dividend policy; both are not inside information.

Breach notification: we assess suspected incidents promptly and notify PDPC and affected individuals as soon as practicable and in any case within 3 calendar days after determining a notifiable data breach, consistent with PDPA Part VIA.

must_detect.matched_text:
Ms. Aisha Rahim, dpo@meridianharbour.sg, +65 6 8 8 8 1 2 3 4, https://meridianharbour.sg/privacy?ref=dsar&id=9a3c-xx12.
Scope under PDPA: personal data means data about an individual who can be identified from that data or from that data and other information to which we have or are likely to have access; business contact information is handled per s.4(5); we treat content already on S G X N E T, press releases, and media briefings as generally available and not MNPI.
DSAR timeline: we target 30 calendar days; we may refuse/limit where disclosure would reveal another individual’s data, confidential legal advice, or work product of Northbanks Law LLC; we will redact where reasonable.
MNPI controls: documents labelled Project Peridot (proposed acquisition of Aurora Quays Pte. Ltd.) are not generally available and may reasonably be expected to materially affect the price or value of Meridian Harbour securities; selective disclosure is prohibited save under duties of confidentiality; Listing Rule 703 requires immediate announcement via S G X - N E T, and we may request a trading halt.
Closed window: Meridian Harbour imposes a 14-day blackout before quarterly results and a 30-day blackout before half-year and full-year results; directors/officers must not deal during the closed period or when in possession of MNPI.
Public-status example: On 2025-02-11 the Board announced via SGXNET (Annct ID: m h d g - 2 5 0 2 - 0 1 1) the divestment of Seabright Logistics (minor subsidiary); this is already public and stale and should not be flagged as non-public information.
Overseas transfers (PDPA s.26): processors in MY/EU/IN engaged under written agreements imposing comparable protection; see https://northbankslaw.sg/transfer?client=mhhl&x=SG26#v=2_0; onward transfers require consent or contract; no cloud move without DPO sign-off.
Retention: deal room materials for M&A and capital-markets matters retained 7 years from completion or abort (whichever later), then securely destroyed; employment files 6 years post-cessation; if NRIC/FIN images are inadvertently captured they must be masked (e.g., S9 9 0 0 0 1 2 A → S*****12A) before storage.
Access controls: MNPI folders (\\meridian\\cf\\peridot) restricted to named team; audit logs preserved; do not paste MNPI into public Teams channels or ticketing tools; lower case project-management prose in stand-ups is not a safe harbor.
DSAR deferral: if an access request covers draft S G X N E T disclosures or board papers prepared for a possible trading halt, we may defer until the information is generally available, then provide a copy with legal privilege redactions.
Non-MNPI examples that may appear in emails and should not trigger escalation: canteen refurbishment scheduling, toner purchases, vendor onboarding for Meridian CloudDesk v2.1 and logistics^app, and notes that explicitly state “no profit guidance” or “no MAC expected for FY2026.”
Public-source references: 2019 Sustainability Report (public) stated a workforce of circa 2,000 and a 12% emissions reduction target; the 2024 Investor Day press release reiterated our dividend policy; both are not inside information.
Breach notification: we assess suspected incidents promptly and notify PDPC and affected individuals as soon as practicable and in any case within 3 calendar days after determining a notifiable data breach, consistent with PDPA Part VIA.
Verification: we will not collect full NRIC numbers for DSAR verification unless permitted by PDPC advisory guidelines; we use layered checks (registered email, last 4 of staff ID, recent payslip ref no. ‘P S L - 2 3 - 7 7’).
Queries may be sent to Mr. Lionel Chua, Compliance, lionel.chua@northbankslaw.sg; Office: 12F, Orion Tower, 88 fict. Shenton Way, Singapore 0 1 8 9 8 8; docref R3T3N-T10N//SG-703?x=peridot%2Fv4
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation
